phase_name: Packaging — Makefile, App Bundle, DMG, First-Launch Setup, and Ollama Check
goal: Produce a distributable `YapTap.app` bundle and `YapTap.dmg` disk image via a `Makefile`. Add the remaining Phase 3 runtime behaviours: bundle-aware resource path resolution, first-launch Python venv setup (with a progress alert), venv-based Python interpreter resolution, Whisper model download warning, and an Ollama availability probe before the LLM step.

in_scope: Makefile with build / icns / app / dmg / clean targets
resources_dir() in config.rs: bundle-aware Resources/ resolution
transcribe_script_path() and llm_script_path() updated to check <resources>/scripts/ first
venv_python_path() and venv_exists() path helpers in config.rs
python_interpreter() in config.rs: returns venv python if present, else 'python3'
run_transcription() and run_llm_collect() updated to use python_interpreter()
run_pipeline_inner() python3 preflight updated to use python_interpreter()
run_setup_commands(): pure subprocess logic for venv creation and pip install
run_first_launch_setup(): UI wrapper showing NSAlert, pumping event loop, handling results
Whisper model download warning written to stderr in transcribe.py before load_model()
ollama_available(): TCP probe to 127.0.0.1:11434 before LLM step
Verification tasks for bundle structure, DMG, first-launch setup, and Ollama check

tasks.title: Create `Makefile` at project root with `build`, `icns`, `app`, `dmg`, and `clean` targets
tasks.description:
Create `Makefile` at the project root (source-controlled). Content must match the spec in `specs/packaging.md` exactly.

- `build`: `cargo build --release`
- `icns`: generates all ten iconset sizes from `assets/icons/yaptap-idle@2x.png` via `sips`, then calls `iconutil -c icns $(ICONSET) -o $(ICNS)`
- `app` (depends on `build icns`): runs `rm -rf $(APP_DIR)`, creates all subdirectories, copies binary, Info.plist, TOML prompts, icon PNGs, Python scripts, and the `.icns` file into `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/`
- `dmg` (depends on `app`): runs `rm -rf $(STAGING_DIR) $(DMG_PATH)`, creates staging dir, copies app, creates `/Applications` symlink, calls `hdiutil create -volname YapTap -srcfolder $(STAGING_DIR) -ov -format UDZO $(DMG_PATH)`, removes staging, prints `Built: $(DMG_PATH)`
- `clean`: `cargo clean && rm -rf $(DIST) $(ICONSET) $(ICNS)`

All five targets declared `.PHONY`. Variable names match the spec: DIST, APP_DIR, CONTENTS, STAGING_DIR, DMG_PATH, SOURCE_ICON, ICONSET, ICNS.
checks: Run `make build` — exit 0 and `target/release/yaptap` exists.
Run `make icns` — `assets/icons/AppIcon.iconset/` contains all ten PNGs and `assets/icons/YapTap.icns` is generated.

Run `make app` — `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/MacOS/yaptap` is executable, `Contents/Info.plist` exists, `Contents/Resources/config/prompts/` contains all TOML files, `Contents/Resources/icons/` contains all four PNGs, `Contents/Resources/scripts/transcribe.py` and `llm.py` exist, `Contents/Resources/YapTap.icns` exists.
Run `make dmg` — `dist/YapTap.dmg` is created and the terminal prints 'Built: dist/YapTap.dmg'.
Run `make clean` — `dist/`, `assets/icons/AppIcon.iconset/`, and `assets/icons/YapTap.icns` are removed.

tasks.title: Create `assets/Info.plist` with all required bundle keys
tasks.description:
Create `assets/Info.plist` (source-controlled; copied into the bundle by `make app`). Required keys and values per `specs/packaging.md`:

| Key | Value |
|-----|-------|
| CFBundleIdentifier | com.yaptap.app |
| CFBundleName | YapTap |
| CFBundleDisplayName | YapTap |
| CFBundleVersion | 0.3.0 |
| CFBundleShortVersionString | 0.3 |
| CFBundleIconFile | YapTap |
| CFBundleExecutable | yaptap |
| CFBundlePackageType | APPL |
| LSUIElement | true (boolean, NOT string) |
| LSMinimumSystemVersion | 12.0 |
| NSHighResolutionCapable | true (boolean) |
| NSMicrophoneUsageDescription | YapTap records audio to transcribe your speech. |
| NSPrincipalClass | NSApplication |
checks: Run `plutil -lint assets/Info.plist` — exit 0 (valid XML plist).
Run `plutil -p assets/Info.plist` and confirm all 13 keys are present with correct values.
Confirm LSUIElement is a boolean `1` (not string `true`) in the plutil output.
Run `make app` and confirm `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Info.plist` is byte-identical to `assets/Info.plist`.

tasks.title: Add `dist/`, `assets/icons/AppIcon.iconset/`, and `assets/icons/YapTap.icns` to `.gitignore`
tasks.description: Append three lines to `.gitignore` (generated artifacts that must not be committed): `dist/`, `assets/icons/AppIcon.iconset/`, `assets/icons/YapTap.icns`. The existing entries (`/target/`, `**/__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`) must be preserved unchanged.
checks: Confirm all three new lines are present in `.gitignore` alongside the existing entries.
Run `make app` then `git status` — `dist/` and the generated icon files do not appear as untracked.
Confirm `assets/Info.plist` and `Makefile` DO appear as untracked (they are source-controlled, not ignored).

tasks.title: Add `pub fn resources_dir() -> PathBuf` to `src/config.rs`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
From project root: `./target/debug/yaptap --list-prompts` succeeds (dev fallback to CWD is used; prompts_dir finds config/prompts/ via updated prompts_dir — see P8-T005).
After `make app`: launch `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/MacOS/yaptap --list-prompts` — prompts listed match `Contents/Resources/config/prompts/` (confirms Resources/ is resolved correctly).

tasks.title: Update `prompts_dir()` in `src/config.rs` to check `resources_dir().join("config/prompts")` first
tasks.description:
The current `prompts_dir()` tries (1) `<binary_dir>/config/prompts` then (2) `<cwd>/config/prompts`. When running from the bundle, `resources_dir()` returns `Contents/Resources/` and the prompts are at `Contents/Resources/config/prompts/`.

Update `prompts_dir()` to try `resources_dir().join("config/prompts")` as the new first candidate, before the existing candidates. Return it immediately if the directory exists. Keep the existing candidates as fallbacks for dev builds.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Dev build from project root: `--list-prompts` still outputs all five prompts (resolves via cwd fallback to `config/prompts/`).
After `make app`: run `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/MacOS/yaptap --list-prompts` — prompts listed are those in `Contents/Resources/config/prompts/` (bundle path used).

tasks.title: Update `transcribe_script_path()` in `src/transcription.rs` to check `resources_dir().join("scripts/transcribe.py")` first
tasks.description:
Prepend `crate::config::resources_dir().join("scripts/transcribe.py")` as the first candidate in `transcribe_script_path()`, before the existing `src/core/` candidates. Use the same `parent().is_dir()` guard that guards existing candidates.

Candidate order after change:
1. `resources_dir().join("scripts/transcribe.py")` — bundle layout (`Contents/Resources/scripts/`)
2. `<binary_dir>/../../src/core/transcribe.py` — dev build at `target/debug/` or `target/release/`
3. `<binary_dir>/src/core/transcribe.py` — legacy installed binary next to `src/`
4. `src/core/transcribe.py` — cwd fallback (project root)

Function signature and return type are unchanged.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Dev build from project root: trigger a recording cycle — transcription succeeds (resolves via candidate 2 to `src/core/transcribe.py`).
After `make app`: trigger a recording cycle from the bundled binary — pipeline does not fail with 'can't open file' for transcribe.py (resolves via candidate 1).

tasks.title: Update `llm_script_path()` in `src/llm.rs` to check `resources_dir().join("scripts/llm.py")` first
tasks.description:
Mirror P8-T006 in `src/llm.rs`: prepend `crate::config::resources_dir().join("scripts/llm.py")` as the first candidate in `llm_script_path()`, before the existing `src/core/` fallbacks. Use the same `parent().is_dir()` guard.

Candidate order after change:
1. `resources_dir().join("scripts/llm.py")` — bundle layout
2. `<binary_dir>/../../src/core/llm.py` — dev build
3. `<binary_dir>/src/core/llm.py` — legacy
4. `src/core/llm.py` — cwd fallback
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Dev build from project root: with a prompt selected, trigger a recording cycle — LLM step succeeds (resolves via candidate 2).
After `make app`: with a prompt selected, trigger a recording cycle from the bundled binary — LLM step does not fail with 'can't open file' for llm.py (resolves via candidate 1).

tasks.title: Add `pub fn venv_python_path() -> PathBuf` to `src/config.rs`
tasks.description:
Add a helper that returns the absolute path to the venv Python interpreter. This is the single canonical definition of the venv path, used by `venv_exists()` (P8-T009) and `python_interpreter()` (P8-T010) so the path string is never duplicated:

```rust
pub fn venv_python_path() -> std::path::PathBuf {
    dirs::home_dir()
        .unwrap_or_else(|| std::path::PathBuf::from("."))
        .join(".config/yaptap/.venv/bin/python")
}
```
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Confirm the returned path is `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/bin/python` (expanded to an absolute path via `dirs::home_dir()`).

tasks.title: Add `pub fn venv_exists() -> bool` to `src/config.rs`
tasks.description:
Add a helper that returns `true` if the venv Python interpreter file exists on disk:

```rust
pub fn venv_exists() -> bool {
    venv_python_path().is_file()
}
```

Used by `run_app()` (P8-T016) to decide whether to run the first-launch setup, and by any other caller that needs a boolean existence check without going through `python_interpreter()`.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/` absent: `venv_exists()` returns `false`.
After first-launch setup (P8-T014/P8-T015) creates the venv: `venv_exists()` returns `true`.

tasks.title: Add `pub fn python_interpreter() -> String` to `src/config.rs`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With venv absent: `python_interpreter()` returns `"python3"`.
With venv present: `python_interpreter()` returns an absolute path ending in `/.venv/bin/python`.

tasks.title: Update `run_transcription()` in `src/transcription.rs` to use `crate::config::python_interpreter()`
tasks.description: Replace `Command::new("python3")` in `run_transcription()` with `Command::new(crate::config::python_interpreter())`. One-line change; all other logic unchanged.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.

With venv present: a recording cycle uses the venv python for transcription (not system python3). Verify by checking that the venv path appears in RUST_LOG=debug output, or by temporarily replacing `python3` in the venv with a shim that logs to a file.

tasks.title: Update `run_llm_collect()` in `src/llm.rs` to use `crate::config::python_interpreter()`
tasks.description: Replace `Command::new("python3")` in `run_llm_collect()` with `Command::new(crate::config::python_interpreter())`. One-line change; all other logic unchanged.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With venv present and a prompt selected: a recording cycle uses the venv python for the LLM step.

tasks.title: Update the python3 preflight in `run_pipeline_inner()` to use `crate::config::python_interpreter()`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With venv present: preflight passes (venv python responds to `--version`).
With no python at all (hypothetical): preflight fails with 'Python interpreter not found: …' message shown as an alert.

tasks.title: Add `fn run_setup_commands() -> Result<(), String>` to `src/app.rs`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Remove `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/`. Call `run_setup_commands()` directly (e.g. via a temporary test main): confirm it returns `Ok(())` and `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/bin/python` exists afterwards.
Simulate failure (temporarily rename python3 or break pip): confirm `Err(String)` is returned with an informative message.

tasks.title: Add `fn run_first_launch_setup()` to `src/app.rs` — UI wrapper for venv setup
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Remove `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/`. Run `./target/debug/yaptap` — the 'Setting up YapTap…' alert window appears within 1 second, with no buttons visible to the user.
After ~30 seconds the alert disappears automatically and the menu bar icon appears.
Confirm `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/bin/python` and `.venv/bin/pip` exist.
With ffmpeg absent: confirm the 'ffmpeg not found' alert appears after setup completes and the app continues to the menu bar icon.
Simulate setup failure (temporarily rename python3): 'Setup failed' alert appears; `.venv/` is not created; app shows menu bar icon (startup continues).

tasks.title: Call `run_first_launch_setup()` from `run_app()` when venv is absent
checks: With venv absent: launching the app triggers the setup alert (confirm ordering: alert appears before the tray icon is visible).
With venv present (second launch): `run_first_launch_setup()` is NOT called; startup proceeds directly to the tray icon.
With another instance already running: `ensure_single_instance()` exits 0 before `run_first_launch_setup()` is reached — no setup alert shown for the duplicate instance.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.

tasks.title: Write Whisper model download warning to stderr in `src/core/transcribe.py` before `load_model()`
tasks.description:
Per `specs/packaging.md`: 'transcribe.py writes a single log line to stderr before loading: "Downloading Whisper base model (~145 MB) — first use only…"'.

In `transcribe()` in `src/core/transcribe.py`, add a warning print to stderr immediately before `whisper.load_model(model_name)`:

```python
print(
    f"Downloading Whisper {model_name} model — first use only (may take 1–3 minutes)…",
    file=sys.stderr,
)
model = whisper.load_model(model_name)
```

The line is written unconditionally before every `load_model()` call. In app mode the Rust binary captures and ignores all stderr output; in CLI mode the user sees it on their terminal, which is useful. The `sys` import is already present at the top of the file.
checks: Confirm the `print(…, file=sys.stderr)` line appears immediately before `whisper.load_model(model_name)` in `transcribe()`.
Run `python3 src/core/transcribe.py <any_wav> 2>&1 | grep 'Downloading'` — confirm the line appears on stderr.
Trigger a full recording cycle in dev mode — confirm the line appears in the terminal (it is not suppressed in CLI mode).
In app mode (from Finder or bundled binary): the line is captured by Rust's stderr pipe and never shown to the user — no change in app behaviour.

tasks.title: Add `fn ollama_available() -> bool` to `src/app.rs`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With Ollama running (`ollama serve`): `ollama_available()` returns `true` within 1 second.
With Ollama stopped: `ollama_available()` returns `false` within ~1 second (connection refused is immediate; timeout only occurs if a firewall drops packets).

tasks.title: Probe Ollama availability before `run_llm_collect()` in `run_pipeline_inner()`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
With Ollama stopped and a prompt selected: press hotkey, speak, stop — 'Ollama not running' alert appears and icon returns to idle.
With 'No Prompt' selected and Ollama stopped: transcription completes normally, raw transcript placed on clipboard — no Ollama alert.
With Ollama running and a prompt selected: pipeline completes normally (no false-positive alert).

tasks.title: Verify `cargo build --release`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` pass clean after all changes
tasks.description: After completing P8-T001 through P8-T019, run a full build, lint, and test pass to confirm no regressions.
checks: Run `cargo build --release` — exit 0.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings, exit 0.
Run `cargo test` — all tests pass.

tasks.title: Verify `make app` produces the correct `dist/YapTap.app` directory structure
tasks.description: Run `make app` and confirm every required file is present in the correct location.
checks: Run `make app` — exit 0.
`file dist/YapTap.app/Contents/MacOS/yaptap` shows 'Mach-O 64-bit executable'.
`plutil -lint dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Info.plist` exits 0.
`ls dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Resources/config/prompts/*.toml` shows all five prompt files.
All four icon PNGs present in `dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Resources/icons/`.
`dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Resources/scripts/transcribe.py` and `llm.py` exist.
`dist/YapTap.app/Contents/Resources/YapTap.icns` exists.
Launch `dist/YapTap.app` from Finder — menu bar icon appears, no crash.

tasks.title: Verify `make dmg` produces a working `dist/YapTap.dmg`
tasks.description: Run `make dmg` and verify the disk image mounts and installs correctly.
checks: Run `make dmg` — exit 0, prints 'Built: dist/YapTap.dmg'.
Mount: `open dist/YapTap.dmg` — Finder shows a volume named 'YapTap' containing `YapTap.app` and an `Applications` symlink.
Drag `YapTap.app` to the Applications shortcut — app copies to `/Applications/YapTap.app`.
Eject the DMG.
Launch `/Applications/YapTap.app` — menu bar icon appears and the full pipeline (hotkey → record → transcribe → clipboard) works.

tasks.title: Verify dev-mode build still works from project root after resource path changes
tasks.description: After P8-T004 through P8-T007 update the resource path resolution, confirm that the development workflow (`./target/debug/yaptap` from the project root) is unaffected. The bundle-first candidates will not match in dev builds, so the existing fallbacks must still resolve correctly.
checks: From the project root, run `./target/debug/yaptap --list-prompts` — confirm all five prompts are listed (prompts_dir resolves via cwd fallback).
Press the hotkey, speak a short phrase, press again — confirm transcription succeeds and transcript is on the clipboard (transcribe_script_path resolves to `src/core/transcribe.py`).
With a prompt selected: repeat the cycle — confirm the LLM step runs (llm_script_path resolves to `src/core/llm.py`).

Run from a different cwd: `cd /tmp && /path/to/target/debug/yaptap --list-prompts` — note whether prompts are found or not (expected: not found from /tmp, which is acceptable — the binary-relative candidate at step 2 in `transcribe_script_path()` should still resolve scripts).

tasks.title: Smoke test first-launch setup from the bundled app
tasks.description: Verify the first-launch Python setup flow end-to-end from `dist/YapTap.app`.
checks: Remove the venv: `rm -rf ~/.config/yaptap/.venv`.
Launch `dist/YapTap.app` from Finder — 'Setting up YapTap…' alert appears within 1 second.
Alert auto-dismisses after ~30 seconds (no user interaction needed).
Confirm `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/bin/python` and `.venv/bin/pip` exist after dismissal.
Tray icon is now visible; press the hotkey, speak, stop — transcription succeeds using venv python (no 'python not found' error).
Quit and relaunch: setup alert does NOT appear (venv already exists); tray icon appears directly.

tasks.title: Smoke test Ollama availability check from the bundled app
tasks.description: Verify the Ollama probe produces the correct alert when Ollama is not running, and is correctly skipped in 'No Prompt' mode.
checks: Ensure Ollama is NOT running (`pkill ollama` or quit the Ollama app).
Select any prompt from the menu bar dropdown.
Press the hotkey, speak a phrase, press again — 'Ollama not running' alert appears; icon returns to idle.
Switch to 'No Prompt'. Press the hotkey, speak, stop — transcription completes and raw transcript is on the clipboard; no Ollama alert shown.
Start Ollama (`ollama serve`), re-select a prompt, repeat the hotkey cycle — LLM step completes and result is on the clipboard.
